Web search on Chinook database schema (schemaspy.org confirms Customer table exists); create_chinook_odb.py line 137 lists 'Customer' in table order
Web search on Chinook database schema confirms Customer table has SupportRepId field as foreign key to Employee.EmployeeId; schemaspy.org/samples/chinook/tables/Customer.html confirms structure

Web search on Chinook database schema confirms Employee table exists with EmployeeId, FirstName, LastName columns; create_chinook_odb.py line 137 lists 'Employee' in table order; schemaspy.org confirms structure
Multiple environment setup scripts reference this path: setup_libreoffice_base.sh lines 65-67, task_utils.sh lines 96, 119, setup_task.sh line 12, export_result.sh line 23

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a form named 'CustomerEntry' in a LibreOffice Base database. The form operates on the Customer table (contains CustomerId, FirstName, LastName, Email, SupportRepId and other fields per Chinook schema). The critical requirement is that the SupportRepId field—which is a foreign key to the Employee table—must use a List Box control displaying employee names (FirstName + LastName concatenated) while storing the EmployeeId value. The Employee table contains 8 rows with EmployeeId as primary key and FirstName, LastName columns. The database is based on the publicly available Chinook sample database (275 artists, 347 albums, 3503 tracks, 59 customers, 412 invoices) converted from SQLite to LibreOffice Base ODB format, located at /home/ga/chinook.odb.
